title: 5 Relationship Red Flags You Should Never Ignore 🚩 II Steve Harvey

body telling you to run. Run, okay?

problem that we always have. Where do you wanna go from here? - Here's red flag number three. (alarm buzzing) If you go out on a
first date with someone, who then immediately wants
to see you the next day, and the next day, and the next day, and he is blowing up your
phone talking about the future, here's the problem. You may not be dealing with a man who is actually head over heels for you. You might just be dealing with someone who is emotionally unavailable
or worse, narcissistic. Most of the time someone who
comes on too fast and furious will probably flame out just as quickly. (audience clapping) - Yeah. Yeah. - On the first several dates, keep the date to about 90 minutes. And if you're having that
great of a time, great. It means you'll both be looking forward to the next date even more because you've left wanting more. - Right.
(audience clapping) What are you thinking
when you hear that, Grace? - I'm thinking recently, I've been on a date that lasted six hours. (audience gasps)
- [Bela] Mm. - And we were talking and having fun, and drinking and laughing, and fast forward to months now, and it's on his term, it's dodgy. He texts me when he wants to text me and- - Well, but you know, what
Bela's saying is true. You gotta be careful about a guy that comes on with everything. He can't keep that pace up, ladies. In 90 days, he'll show you who he is. (audience clapping) That leads me to the fourth red flag. What do you got? - The fourth red flag is related to something
you just mentioned earlier. Dodgy.
(alarm buzzing) The word is inconsistency. If a man goes from
texting you all the time, taking you out consistently, calling you, but suddenly he's now too busy, right? He doesn't call you, he
doesn't text you as much, he doesn't take you out as much. His feelings have changed. Nobody is too

what he's not looking for, or what he's not attracted to, again, he is telling you and showing
